perturbation.original_text: NON- DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Agreement effective ___________ between MARIPOSA LABS LLC , an Idaho corporation (“ MARIPOSA”) and ___________________ (“Company”).
perturbation.changed_text: NON- DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Agreement effective ___________ between MARIPOSA LABS LLC , an Idaho corporation (“ MARIPOSA”) and ___________________ (“Client”).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' is replaced with 'Client'. This creates ambiguity as the rest of the document refers to 'Company', and it is not explicitly stated that 'Company' and 'Client' are the same, thus introducing a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Definition of Confidentiality Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean the formulas and related information supplied t o MARIPOSA by the Company but only to the extent that: (a) Such information is maintained as confidential by the Company, and; (b) Marked or otherwise identified as confidential when disclosed to MARIPOSA. Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall be treated as Confidential Information if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Client' in the agreement's definition. The contradicted text refers to 'Company' providing confidential information. Because the change makes it unclear whether 'Client' and 'Company' refer to the same entity, a contradiction arises. It introduces ambiguity and potential conflict because obligations tied to 'Company' are no longer clearly applicable to 'Client', as there is no guarantee they are the same. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: MARIPOSA is a manufacturer located in Garden City, Idaho and manufactures a wide range of personal care products. The Company has requested that MARIPOSA exchange confidential information in connection with product formulation, which may or may not lead to manufacturing of said product.
perturbation.changed_text: MARIPOSA is a manufacturer located in Garden City, Idaho and manufactures a wide range of personal care products. The Customer has requested that MARIPOSA exchange confidential information in connection with product formulation, which may or may not lead to manufacturing of said product.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Company' to 'Customer' without defining it as the same entity could lead to confusion about which party is bound to the obligations defined for 'Company' later in the agreement. This creates ambiguity in section 2.2 where 'Company' is mentioned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Restricted Use. Confidential Information provided by the Company in accordance with this Agreement is for MARIPOSA’s use in the manufacture of the Company’s products only. MARIPOSA shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information other than as reasonably necessary to acco mplish these purposes. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Company understands and agrees as follows: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation; (b) MARIPOSA’s nonuse and nondiscl osure obligations above shall not apply to such Confidential Information as: (1) was publicly known prior to disclosure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (2) became publicly known, without fault on the part of MARIPOSA, subsequent to disclos ure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (3) was received by MARIPOSA at any time from a source, other than the Company, lawfully having possession of the right to disclose such information; or (4) was otherwise known by MARIPOSA prior to disclo sure by the Company to MARIPOSA of such information; (c) The product formulas used for the Company products may be similar to formulas used by other manufacturers, and it is unlikely that any such formulas would qualify for patent or other intellectual property protection; (d) MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall not be considered identical if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company' to 'Customer' in the Changed Text introduces ambiguity because Section 2.2, the Contradicted Text, specifically refers to 'Company' and defines MARIPOSA's obligations regarding the confidential information of 'the Company'. By changing 'Company' to 'Customer' without clarification, it's unclear if the obligations in Section 2.2 still apply to the 'Customer', creating a conflict and uncertainty about MARIPOSA's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2.  CONFIDENTIALITY 2.1 Definition of Confidentiality Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean the formulas and related information supplied t o MARIPOSA by the Company but only to the extent that: (a) Such information is maintained as confidential by the Company, and; (b) Marked or otherwise identified as confidential when disclosed to MARIPOSA. Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall be treated as Confidential Information if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.  CONFIDENTIALITY 2.1 Definition of Confidentiality Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean the formulas and related information supplied t o MARIPOSA by the Organization but only to the extent that: (a) Such information is maintained as confidential by the Organization, and; (b) Marked or otherwise identified as confidential when disclosed to MARIPOSA. Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall be treated as Confidential Information if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Company' to 'Organization' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' causes the definition to contradict with the subsequent clauses. The rest of the agreement then uses Company, while 2.1 says it should be supplied by the Organization. This introduces uncertainty about whose information is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Restricted Use. Confidential Information provided by the Company in accordance with this Agreement is for MARIPOSA’s use in the manufacture of the Company’s products only. MARIPOSA shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information other than as reasonably necessary to acco mplish these purposes. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Company understands and agrees as follows: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation; (b) MARIPOSA’s nonuse and nondiscl osure obligations above shall not apply to such Confidential Information as: (1) was publicly known prior to disclosure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (2) became publicly known, without fault on the part of MARIPOSA, subsequent to disclos ure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (3) was received by MARIPOSA at any time from a source, other than the Company, lawfully having possession of the right to disclose such information; or (4) was otherwise known by MARIPOSA prior to disclo sure by the Company to MARIPOSA of such information; (c) The product formulas used for the Company products may be similar to formulas used by other manufacturers, and it is unlikely that any such formulas would qualify for patent or other intellectual property protection; (d) MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall not be considered identical if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Company' with 'Organization' in the definition of Confidential Information in Section 2.1. However, Section 2.2, the contradicted text, refers to 'Confidential Information provided by the Company'. This creates a contradiction because Section 2.1 now defines Confidential Information as being supplied by the 'Organization', while Section 2.2 states it's provided by the 'Company', leading to ambiguity about whose information is protected and conflicting obligations, satisfying the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation; (b) MARIPOSA’s nonuse and nondiscl osure obligations above shall not apply to such Confidential Information as: (1) was publicly known prior to disclosure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (2) became publicly known, without fault on the part of MARIPOSA, subsequent to disclos ure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (3) was received by MARIPOSA at any time from a source, other than the Company, lawfully having possession of the right to disclose such information; or (4) was otherwise known by MARIPOSA prior to disclo sure by the Company to MARIPOSA of such information;
perturbation.changed_text: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation; (b) MARIPOSA’s nonuse and nondiscl osure obligations above shall not apply to such Confidential Information as: (1) was publicly known prior to disclosure by the Business of such information to MARIPOSA; (2) became publicly known, without fault on the part of MARIPOSA, subsequent to disclos ure by the Business of such information to MARIPOSA; (3) was received by MARIPOSA at any time from a source, other than the Business, lawfully having possession of the right to disclose such information; or (4) was otherwise known by MARIPOSA prior to disclo sure by the Business to MARIPOSA of such information;
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Company' with 'Business' introduces a contradiction, particularly since the governing definition of 'Confidential Information' in 2.1 refers back to the Company, but this section is saying the confidential information is from the Business. This makes it unclear to whom the exceptions to non-disclosure apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Definition of Confidentiality Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean the formulas and related information supplied t o MARIPOSA by the Company but only to the extent that: (a) Such information is maintained as confidential by the Company, and; (b) Marked or otherwise identified as confidential when disclosed to MARIPOSA. Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall be treated as Confidential Information if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to 'Company' which aligns with section 2.1's definition of 'Confidential Information' as being supplied by 'the Company'. The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Business'. Section 2.1 specifically defines Confidential Information as that provided by 'the Company'. Therefore, the change introduces a contradiction because the exceptions to MARIPOSA's non-disclosure obligations now refer to information received from the 'Business', while the definition of 'Confidential Information' refers to information supplied by 'the Company'. This creates a direct conflict making it unclear to which entity the exceptions apply, violating the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (c) The product formulas used for the Company products may be similar to formulas used by other manufacturers, and it is unlikely that any such formulas would qualify for patent or other intellectual property protection; (d) MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall not be considered identical if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) The product formulas used for the Enterprise products may be similar to formulas used by other manufacturers, and it is unlikely that any such formulas would qualify for patent or other intellectual property protection; (d) MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Enterprise products. In this regard, a formula shall not be considered identical if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Company' to 'Enterprise' here creates ambiguity. The earlier sections define the scope of confidentiality with regards to information from 'The Company', and it is unclear if 'Enterprise' products are afforded the same protections or considerations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Definition of Confidentiality Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean the formulas and related information supplied t o MARIPOSA by the Company but only to the extent that: (a) Such information is maintained as confidential by the Company, and; (b) Marked or otherwise identified as confidential when disclosed to MARIPOSA. Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall be treated as Confidential Information if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company' to 'Enterprise' introduces ambiguity regarding the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 2.1. Section 2.1 explicitly defines Confidential Information as that provided by 'the Company'. By changing 'Company' to 'Enterprise', it's unclear whether the formulas and related information from 'Enterprise' are also covered by the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 2.1. This creates a direct conflict and misaligns the terminology, potentially leading to disputes over which information is protected, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. SIGNATURES MARIPOSA LABS LLC Dated: ________ ________ By: __________________________________________ Milt Gillespie, President COMPANY Dated: _________________ By: ___________________________________________ (Authorized Signature) _ _____________________________________ (Print or Type Name of Authorized Signature)
perturbation.changed_text: 4. SIGNATURES MARIPOSA LABS LLC Dated: ________ ________ By: __________________________________________ Milt Gillespie, President Organization Dated: _________________ By: ___________________________________________ (Authorized Signature) _ _____________________________________ (Print or Type Name of Authorized Signature)
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'COMPANY' to 'Organization', the signature block now reflects 'Organization', contradicting the rest of the document, which refers to 'Company'. This could raise doubts about the validity and the real party agreeing to this contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON- DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Agreement effective ___________ between MARIPOSA LABS LLC , an Idaho corporation (“ MARIPOSA”) and ___________________ (“Company”). 1. BACKGROUND MARIPOSA is a manufacturer located in Garden City, Idaho and manufactures a wide range of personal care products. The Company has requested that MARIPOSA exchange confidential information in connection with product formulation, which may or may not lead to manufacturing of said product. The purpose of this Agreement is to detail the rights of the parties with respect to confidential information that may be provided between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'COMPANY' in the signature block. The changed text replaces 'COMPANY' with 'Organization'. The contradicted text defines one of the parties as 'Company'. By changing 'COMPANY' to 'Organization', the signature block now reflects 'Organization', contradicting the rest of the document, which refers to 'Company'. This creates a misaligned terminology as defined above, potentially raising doubts about the validity and the real party agreeing to this contract, thus there is a strong in-text contradiction.
